attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches in final 100-500m, often committing very late to exploit hesitation. Capable of winning from deep positions (25th at 800m) when leadout fails, showing exceptional raw acceleration.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on Quick-Step/Deceuninck leadout train with MØRKØV, RICHEZE, or SABATINI as final pilot. Team controls finale but coordination frequently breaks down in final kilometer, forcing improvised sprints from poor positions.
terrain_preference: Pure flat sprints suit best; demonstrated evolved altitude tolerance (won at 3000m). Completely exposed on any climb above 4% gradient or technical uphill finales where positioning errors compound.
vulnerability: Chronic positioning indecision in final 2km—repeatedly checks over shoulder, abandons own leadout train, or follows wrong wheels. Dropped reliably on climbs 6-7km before summit. Crashes frequently at intermediate sprints contesting points.
key_patterns: Positioning indecision: wastes leadout by checking shoulders or switching wheels mid-sprint (5+ instances)
Leadout coordination breakdown: disconnects from own train in final kilometer despite Quick-Step numerical presence (7+ instances)
Dropped predictably 6-7km before climb summits, tactical positioning failure rather than fitness (3 instances)
Crashes or loses position contesting intermediate sprints, compromising stage finale (4 instances)
Can win from catastrophic positions (25th at 800m) when forced to improvise, showing superior raw power
Stage wins cluster in Giro d'Italia (4 wins 2018), struggles at Tour de France with power deficit
form_trajectory: negative: 2021 shows decisiveness issues (hesitated vs MERLIER despite SABATINI leadout), barely top-10 finishes, and emotional distractions. Clear decline from 2018-2019 dominance.
